$comment: C14 (C 6.7.9p14 + 6.2.5p15, D-CSUBSET-STRING-LITERAL-ARRAY-ZERO-FILL extended to the signed/unsigned character types): a string literal may initialize an array of ANY character type — plain char, signed char (I8), unsigned char (U8). Before C14 only plain char[N]='…' was admitted; `unsigned char[N]='…'` / `signed char[N]='…'` raised S0003 and the aggregate form H_VerifierFailure. The real sqlite shape: test_hexio `const unsigned char zHex[]='0123456789ABCDEF'` + test_func charmaps. The `hex`/`sc` arrays are LOCALS (runtime byte-wise init from the retyped rodata literal), so the release arm exercises the real init copy + loads, not a folded constant; the aggregate `unsigned char zTag[7]` field exercises the aggregate producer's zero-fill. SENSITIVE: reads the bytes (correct?) AND the trailing zero pad (zero?) — a wrong pad or OOB read → a distinct non-42 exit at the failing byte; exit 42 == every byte verified. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the C14 widening (stringLiteralArrayInitCompatible back to char-on-both-sides) → `unsigned char hex[18]='…'` / `signed char sc[6]='abc'` → S0003; the aggregate rows → H_VerifierFailure. Pure c-subset → all 4 targets; the pe target RUNS on Windows. The release arm runs the shipped pipeline over the retyped rodata globals + the byte-wise local-init copies.
